six day trip in Peru and we did so much from the people we met to the culture to the amazing food the Landscapes the world wonders we did so much but in this video we're gonna give you guys 14 things that shocked us on our trip to Peru so stay tuned you don't want to miss this one so number one the seasons are completely flipped around if you're coming from the United States so we planned this trip during our summer during the months of June and July so leaving from New York it was about 90 degrees and a heat wave here but we went down to Peru and it was completely flipped around it's their winter so Peru is located in the southern hemisphere so our summer is actually summer is also their dry season so there actually wasn't any rain during the time that we were there which is fantastic for us at least so the next thing that shot this was that there was a major weather changes from location to location in Peru and this really ties into where Peru is located in the southern hemisphere in South America so Lima Peru is actually a coastal city so you get a lot of Breeze coming in from the ocean but at the same time it is low elevation so it still was kind of hot when we were there think for my people who know Fahrenheit around 70s or with the Sun out high 70s so it really varies depending on when the sun is and where the shade is as well but going from Lima to Cusco was a major difference especially with that Sun so Cusco is located in the Andes mountains and when the sun is not there it is cold I don't mean chili I mean cold like we had to go and buy ponchos because it was so cold when we went during the day when the sun is out it was pretty warm though we didn't need the ponchos but at night get those ponchos you need the layers so even though Cusco is on the cooler side Machu Picchu has its own climate so we went there with our ponchos trying to take some picturesque pictures there but we had to take those off quickly expeditiously because walking around hiking season yes it's especially true if you're coming during a very sunny day we'll actually talk about our experience with Machu Picchu a little bit later on in the video so stay tuned for that as well the next thing that surprised us was the changes in altitude throughout the country so as we said earlier Lima is basically at sea level with not a lot of elevation however when you fly into Cusco the altitude before you go back to Cusco later on all right so the next thing is that Peru has some of the best food in the whole world Peru is one of the foodie capitals of the world and every all organized by budget so check that out guys how Packers were super cute and cuddly but they are not to be confused with a llama so alpacas are typically found at higher elevations but they were actually brought to Machu Picchu to mow the lawn they are natural lawn mowers you can see a lot of them in Cusco on the streets and you can actually take pictures with them just be sure to bargain and the alpaca just be sure to pet uh alpaca right after just to ease the guilt but fantastic eat the alpaca pet the alpaca the next thing I shocked us was that there were so many Ubers available and they there is a ton of traffic in Lima in particular so in these instances public transportation may be a better bet but we personally did not use it the buses go in their own lane so they typically just bypass the traffic so different experience and a lot more affordable but I mean 50 cents versus a dollar is neither here nor there for us personally as privileged Americans so the next thing that really shocked us was how affordable and well connected Peru is through buses so we took different buses to get to different cities in Peru and they were fantastic a lot of them had a really nice incline chairs they were comfortable and they had assigned seating so I knew I would sit next to my wife and our overall experience with the buses was safe and comfortable so we took buses to the ballistics Islands ICA as well as wakachina which was an amazing day trip and we took public transportation the whole way you can see our experience linked below and in the top right corner as well so the next thing that surprised us was that credit cards are widely accepted in Peru so we traveled over 10 countries in Latin America and a lot of these countries they typically don't take credit card but Peru was great because they did so their actual currency is the sole and at the time of this recording it's a little bit under four soles to one US dollar so while we did have cash on us that we did use using our credit cards that have no foreign transaction fees was super beneficial for us to save on our cash so the next thing that was really shocking was just how affordable Peru was as an American so overall your currency goes really far with the US dollar and I'm talking about we had amazing airbnbs for under thirty dollars a night we had a beachfront Airbnb with two bedrooms two In Our description below so the next thing that surprised us was that usually we like to do things DIY but at Machu Picchu you pretty much do need a guide so local guide will give you perspective to literally what you're looking at because if you don't have a guide you're just looking at rocks and grass and mountains now while that is beautiful you need those contacts and the whole story behind it so much to the story so many details that you really need to know about so our guide was top tier he spoke really great English he took amazing photos and he let us go at our own pace and gave us so much great insight into Machu Picchu so we have his contact information included in our explore Peru guide as well the next thing that's the process was that when you enter Peru you actually don't get a passport stamp so we want to get as many stamps as possible we want to fill up our little passports but there is a place in agos Calientes where you can get a stamp of Machu Picchu in your passport however just be cognizant that this is not an official stamp and some places may consider your passport damage by putting unofficial stamps in your passport the next thing that was shocking was all the different fives that were in Lima so as we said we went in there winter and we stayed three different days in Lima and every day was a different Adventure even though we were there with art we already talked about the amazing Foods the differences in culture the spanish-style buildings the old Styles every different area in Lima is completely different from the others so the next thing that we want to cover is safety and how we felt while we were in Peru so while we were there I usually walk around with my little camera and a lot of people told me to watch out and be careful with walking around with my camera now while I do understand where people are coming from and definitely take their information serious I did not feel unsafe during any point in my vacation and this is just my experience from going around in Lima Peru and this is including not being a fluent Spanish speaker taking public transportation walking around a lot and having technology I did not feel unsafe at all but still you should be aware of your surroundings and if something feels off don't stick around the next thing that shouldn't actually surprise you is that most people speak Spanish so definitely brush up on your Spanish before heading to Peru another great thing is an a lot of high traffic tourist areas those people tend to speak a lot of good English the menus typically are in Spanish and English in those areas as well and your best bet if you're not fluent in Spanish is to use Google Translate download those offline apps and they even have a Google translate photo option videos.transcript: talk about things to do in Cusco and a visit to Machu Picchu is always the first to come to mind of course a visit to this ancient Incan Citadel is a must but there are many other things to do during your stay in this big city well here are some of the top things to do in Cusco and you should stick around until the end to discover all the Fantastic activities listed in this video number one see the 12 angled Stone the ancient city of Cusco is known for many things but it is specifically famous for its unique structures of course the Incan civilization was known for its architectural prowess that featured perfectly cut stones and the 12 angled Stone of a tun Rumi Oak is arguably the best of the lot lauded for its perfect assembly it's said that not even a sheet of paper can fit between the stones over the years this archaeological artifact has become a popular tourist attraction drawing many visitors from far and wide in fact this piece of stone is considered a National Heritage object so once you settle in at your Cusco accommodation you may want to step out to atunurumi Oak Street where you will be wowed By the Fantastic 12 angled Stone number two visit the korikancha temple the kodekancha temple or the Temple of Light as it translates was arguably the most important structure of the Incan Empire the walls of this Holy Temple are covered with gold plates and it is one of the most impressive structures in Cusco it also features a Christian Monastery built on its top by the Spanish in an attempt to change the religion in these parts today the temple is still standing and has become one of the top tourist attractions in Cusco of course there are many excellent structures in this region but a visit to Cory kansa Temple is one you don't want to miss number three eat the San Pedro Market no doubt Peru is blessed with lots of local recipes and you do not want to leave without indulging your taste buds in these Delight full local dishes luckily you can get the best of these dishes here in Cusco and the San Pedro Market is where you should go to enjoy exciting local recipes the San Pedro Market was initially a hot spot downtown where you can quickly get whatever you need but it is also grown to become the most famous for the local dishes served here the meals here are said to be cheaper and tastier than in the restaurants across town and you can often see locals and tourists alike lining up to eat here if you wouldn't mind having your taste buds blown you should try two or three Delicacies here at the San Pedro Market number four take a photo with a baby llama one common scene you will surely encounter in your walk across the streets of Cusco is the costumed women carrying baby llamas this is quite a unique scene and you will surely find the combination photogenic sometimes instead of the women you could see little girls in these costumes with baby llamas and they will demand you to pay a small tip in exchange for a photo with the llamas well since this is an awe firing City you sure don't want to miss out on taking the photo and saving this unique memory number five party it out at Wild Rover and Mama Africa the nightlife in Cusco is electric complementing the numerous attractions you can explore during the day of course one of the best ways to spend your evenings in Cusco is to step out to a club or bar and party it out with the locals and other tourists like you Mama Africa is one of the famous nightclubs in this park and you can rest assured of a fun evening dancing to local Peruvian music and meeting new and exciting people alternatively you can head down to The Wild Rover hotel to enjoy a similar experience even if you are not a very good dancer you will find friendly locals open to teaching you a few dance moves to get you in on the fun you definitely do not want to leave Cusco without exploring these nightclubs number six have a beer at the world's highest irish-owned Pub if you want to take a break from cusco's local recipes you should head down to Paddy's Irish Pub where you can get a taste of Ireland's food and beer combination located just outside Plaza de armas Paddy's Pub is world renowned for being the highest irish-owned Pub and this is thanks to cusco's 3399 meter altitude Patty's Pub is known for its maximum height and has also made a name in these parts for the Delicacies on its menu you can enjoy a variety of food like shepherd's pie and Pizza in this Pub or you can choose to have a beer or two while relaxing with friends or enjoying the company of locals number seven check out San Blas neighborhood if you would like to wander away from the crowded areas of Cusco but still enjoy a fun time out the Nissan Bloss neighborhood is where you should head to this picture-esque neighborhood is not too far away from Plaza de armas and it is quickly becoming a very popular patio here here you will find the best restaurants boutiques and find cafes open to serve interested locals and tourists this district is not the most tour Forest populated but it often comes alive on weekends and that's the best time to come check out the San Blas neighborhood of course this is one place you don't want to miss out on visiting during your stay in Cusco number eight wander around cusco's Historical Center if you are a history Enthusiast visiting cusco's Historical Center is non-negotiable as you will get to explore the best Spanish and and Dayan cultures alongside the parts they play in Peru's Rich history conveniently located toward the city center this District often attracts tourists from far and wide the main Square here is the famous Plaza de armas and it is one of the most popular in all of Peru here you'll find many historic monuments and iconic buildings like the Cusco Cathedral and they are sure to take your breath away also there are many quality restaurants and bars here and you already know you are up for a good time wandering the streets of this historic Center by the way if you want to learn how to save money on airfares then click the link in the description below this guy guide will teach you how to always get the cheapest flight number nine visit the statue of Christ after settling here in Cusco one of the things to do next is head out on a visit to Cristo Blanco which translates to the Statue of the white Christ at about eight meters tall it is not as big as the statue in Rio but it is certainly one of the dominant statues in Cusco tourists visiting this part of the country often have a visit to Cristo Blanco on the top of their list of things to do in Cusco and you don't want to miss out on the opportunity either if you are visiting with your family this is one of the best locations to have a picnic and you can find other locals doing the same you'll get to enjoy the Fantastic scenery so make sure to take pictures to remember this experience number 10 celebrate the spectacular inti Raimi Festival if you are visiting Cusco in Winter you may be lucky enough to witness the spectacular inti Rami Festival the Festival of the Sun as it translates was the most important of the four festivals celebrated by the Incas and it was to honor and celebrate the return of the sun god the festival is celebrated on the 24th of June which is the day when the sun is furthest from the earth in these parts plus it also marks the Inca New Year of course the Incan Empire is no more and the civilization has since become obsolete but the inti Raimi Festival is still very much celebrated in Cusco and you can always join in on the celebration on the Festival date thousands of locals and tourists March along the streets of the Historical Center and congregate at the esplantes to witness the cultural activities carried out to relive the Inca Legacy you will surely enjoy it number 11. visit pachacutec Museum Cusco is very much known for the numerous historical museums here and you may want to relive history by visiting a few of them wondering where to start well the pachukwu Tech Museum is one of the best in the region built in honor of pachikutek who was the greatest Incan ruler the museum has seven floors each dedicated to different different stages of The Emperor's life and you'll undoubtedly find them pretty interesting other museums to check out in Cusco include the choco Museum where you can learn about the history of cacao and the production process of chocolate if you find this place fascinating then wait till you get to the Papa Museum which is dedicated to the history of potatoes in the country Peru has about 4 000 varieties of potatoes so it's only ideal to have a Potato Museum in Cusco that teaches the cultivation process of each species number 12 explore the success woman ruins another exciting activity in Cusco for history lovers entails visiting the various ruins here of course Machu Picchu is the main attraction in this region but there are other minor ruins on the outskirts of Cusco that are worth visiting and success woman tops the list formerly a major Citadel built by the kilke culture and expanded by the Incas the massive Stone structure has since been reduced to ruins but then it still retains its status as as a public attraction in the area a bit further away from Cusco lies the kenko ruins which used to be a temple built with limestone rocks and you may want to include it in your list of ruins to visit during your stay in Cusco other ruins worth visiting in these parts include the puka bukata which is also known as the red Fortress and tumbo machai which was formerly a site for ritual bathing tourists usually visit these few ruins together as part of the Cusco tourist ticket circuit number 13. soak in the beauty and history of the Sacred Valley talk about the best day trips from Cusco and the sacred Valley is a close second behind the famous Machu Picchu it features a range of valleys Rivers towns and other well-preserved Inca architecture surely you should head out to the sacred Valley to soak in the beauty and Rich history here urubamba is the largest town in these parts and it lies along the road to Machu Picchu just beside its namesake Lake and it is a commercial hub that attracts tourists making their way here oyentambo is another tourist Village in the valley and it features some breathtaking archaeological sites built by the Incans after their Victory against the Spaniards maras is another town to explore when you get here and it's known for its salt mines this network of over a thousand cascading pools of ancient salt deposits has all it takes to be the highlight of your trip here as it promises to leave you amazed from here you can also head down to The mordai Terraces to climax your visit at the sacred Valley with the site of Amphitheater Terraces these Terraces are believed to have been used as agricultural Labs where the Incans performed several experiments resulting in the discovery of thousands of potato species number 14. hike up to the Rainbow Mountain hiking is one activity you want to take advantage of during your visit to Cusco of course there are exciting peaks in this part of the country but none is as unique and awe-inspiring as the famous Rainbow Mountain this mystical seven-colored Mountain used to be covered in ice and was only discovered about a decade ago due to climate change in the area however it is quickly becoming one of the top tourist attractions in the area attracting over 1 000 visitors daily here visitors often have to hike a narrow part of 5200 meters to get to the Mountaintop if you reckon yourself fit enough you can join a trekking party at the bottom of the mountain if not you can rent a horse to take you up to this fantastic Mountain to experience the seven colored Wonder up close number 15 Marvel at the beauty of the umantai lake South America is responsible for many great Glacier lakes and mountains and you want to take advantage of your time here to go explore some of them well if you are in Cusco then you are not far away from umantai which is one of the most beautiful Glacier lakes in the world the Crystal Blue Lake is situated over 2 000 meters up the umantai glacier mountain and often Sparkles whenever the Sun comes out plus if you are are up for a little more Adventure you can climb higher up the ridge from whence you will be regarded with a Sublime view of the lake and surrounding scenery luckily most tour guides in Cusco recommend this trip which is hard to miss anyway number 16. go on a multi-day trek depending on how long you will be staying in the tourist city of Cusco you may want to include one or two multi-day treks on your list of things to do luckily there are more than a few destinations to explore on a multi-day hike from Cusco the top of this list is the famous Inca Trail and it is one you don't want to miss this Trail offers one of the most popular treks on the continent and it features two versions of the Trek the classic Inca Trail is the main version laced with more tourist attractions to keep you entertained throughout the Trek on the other hand the short Inca Trail offers a shorter route to Machu Picchu but it is often less entertaining than the classic Trail the salcantai trail is another very popular Trail in Cusco often taken as a an alternative to the classic Inca Trail the trail passes through the top of the salkantai mountain and often requires a level of determination and resilience to complete the Inca Trail may be the most famous here but the salkanti trail is certainly no pushover and it even features on most lists of top 25 treks in the world other exceptional tricks to embark on from Cusco include the lares choke akirao and aosangate Trex which offer unique experiences for sure these treks are all worth a place in your Cusco to-do list and they all promise an experience to remember number 17 take the train to Machu Picchu well at the end of the day your tour in Cusco is certainly not complete without a trip to the famous Machu Picchu and this is one activity to highlight on your bucket list indeed there are many ways to get to Machu Picchu but the train is by far the most popular and exciting this is helped by the fact that it is the only public transport to Machu teach you however it is an experience you will not forget in a hurry the train passes through several fascinating sites before arriving at the ancient Incan Citadel undoubtedly a ride on the train to Machu Picchu will convince you that the experience is worth every height click on our next video now to discover many other fascinating details about Peru

vendors selling food alcohol and all sorts of household essentials you'll also find tons of amazing restaurants inside selling fresh and authentic peruvian foods another thing that's great about the san pedro market is it's right next to the peru rail train station so if you're planning on visiting machu picchu this is a great spot to load up on snacks before that four-hour train ride we opted to stay at the kuwait hostel which is right near this market and right near the train station it was a cheap and enjoyable place to stay we'll link it down below if you're interested the next spot on our list is san francisco plaza san francisco plaza is a beautiful square in the historic downtown area you can reach this plaza by walking through the arco santa clara which is a beautiful arc that connects the san pedro market area to the square we're on the plaza san francisco it's really really tranquil here there's lots of people sitting on the benches enjoying the park the architecture here is phenomenal behind me we have this beautiful church with beautiful bell towers if you're looking versus a small little escape in cuzco this is definitely it and while you're near the san francisco plaza the next spot you'll need to check out is plaza de armas this is the main public square in cusco with history dating back to the 1500s plaza de armas is filled with lavish fountains statues and grand architecture on a sunny day it's absolutely majestic this is a really lively part of the downtown with beautiful churches and shops to explore so we're in the plaza de armas now this is gorgeous it was the chosen starting point for the imperial city which later became the capital of the inca empire yeah and now it's just this beautiful square lots of people selling artwork everyone here's been totally respectful you're not being heckled or pulled in to buy things you're flanked on all sides by beautiful churches gorgeous architecture really really cool spot in cuzco the next cusco icon you need to see is cristo blanco this giant statue can be seen from all over the city and if you don't feel like taking a tour bus to the top the walk to the statue takes about 30 minutes if you start in the city center of plaza de armas and while google maps says it's 30 minutes be sure to allow a little bit more time for this because the hike up is exhausting we just made it up those steps and we have these to go we're getting our cardio in today so what's kind of cool is with every step we take we're at the highest elevation we've ever been in our lives cusco has an elevation of about 11 000 feet and we've only been to denver before which we just looked it up it's only about 5 000 feet so this is more than double the altitude of denver and we're really feeling it so there's only two things that are making up for these really tough stairs one the views and two all the little pups that are following us along the way but definitely don't underestimate these stairs when you reach the top you'll be greeted with one of the best views of the city so we're at cristo blanco right now the statue of christ which sits on top of cusco and you get an amazing view of the entire city from up here the plaza de armas here the altitude is no joke it was really exhausting us getting up here while you're near crystal blanco it's the perfect time to check out the next top attraction in cusco and that's the kenko chico ruins while many of the inca sites around cusco require a paid tourist ticket these runes are free and you can explore at your own pace and since you're probably going to be spending a lot of money on your machu picchu trip we appreciated being able to save our pennies a little bit here and another bonus is being off the tourist circuit you can expect to have much smaller crowds while exploring these incan ruins oh careful this architecture is astounding you can just see how every stone meshes so perfectly no use of mortar yeah it's kind of sad in the 21st century i don't think i could figure this out it's amazing that this was done hundreds of years ago seriously guys you have to come here i feel like i'm straight out of indiana jones having all these ruins to explore by ourselves this is what peru is all about so if you venture downward a little further from the chico section of the ruins you can see there's some more ruins here but we're guessing these seemed like there was an admission cost but we could see plenty just from walking around it and it was really cool near the kenko chico ruins is the next top spot in cusco that you'll need to check out and that is sakse wuma these are probably the most famous incan ruins around cuzco and is a large fortress full of incan stonework to actually enter these ruins you'll need to purchase a cusco tourist ticket which costs between 70 and 130 soles depending on what attractions you wish to see so even if you don't go into the sexy bomb in ruins it's actually really cool to see it from the I solo travelled to Peru in May 2023 and the question I get asked most is: is it safe to travel Peru? Watch my travel vlog to find out! lunch is Jack's Cafe I often see a queue outside and they sell traditional breakfast food in big portions so if you're hungry head here and if you want lunch with great views head to limbus Resto bar it's a bit of a Relentless climb but quite glorious once you're up there but Christian what if I just want a really nice coffee and cake where should I go I got you my friends I'm a fan of the cool calm coffee shops with local Peruvian coffee Blends and Good Vibes my faves being three monkeys coffee Cusco they have a gorgeous little Courtyard to enjoy a coffee and a cake also Florencia e for donara Cafe is a lovely spot to retreat if you're up on the hill you can get a great coffee at Barrio Cafe it's tiny but cute and good coffee and then I'd also recommend Cafe duazi close to Plaza de armas for a great local blend in a more traditional coffee shop setting but we're not done there when I tell you Cusco has something to suit your every wish and occasion I really mean it if you just want some kind of dessert the first place you can head is kucharitas they have all the dessert options that you can think of to satisfy your craving then just across the way you also have La Casa de los crepes organicos aka the creperia this guy knows how to make a banging crap or for a more traditional Peruvian dessert you can get pikarones at picarones it's like a syrupy donut but made with sweet potato and squash you gotta try it so you've stuffed yourself to the max with delicious food and now you want to go out for a drink I got you my friends once again Cusco has a bar for every occasion Peru is of course famous for the Pisco Sour and and arguably the best one in town you can get at Republica Del Pisco of course they have a really great live music in here but it's also super loud so don't expect to get in a good conversation but it is good vibes the other place to go for live music is Berlin they often have live salsa music in here which is really fun if you want to salsa with someone my favorite place to go for some pre-drinks was La chupederia aka the shop bar they have great deals on all of their cocktails and they are strong the music they play in there is also so fun and they also just literally give you shots for fun and then right next door you have Los Perros which I also really like it's a bit more low-key it's filled with comfy seats so you can get a bit Cozier but still a fairly Lively Vibe but if you do want something a lot classier and low-key I recommend the rossoba which is the cheese and wine bar you sit on high chairs around these wine barrels and sip delicious wine and they give you bite-sized cheese chunks to nibble on I really like it in there it's a great date setting as is black cat just next door it's a tiny bar but it's chill and classy and comfy sofas and the cocktails are amazing and if you're ready to head to the club changos is the place it's the biggest Club in town there is often a queue outside you may need to pay between 10 to 30 dollars for entry depending on the day and time but this is where everyone ends up or if you're not a fan of pop music there's actually a techno club much smaller much more secret it's really for the Techno fans it's called casatisok and as for the general tips in Cusco really be careful on the cobbled stones in Cusco they are absolute absolutely Relentless I rolled over my ankle twice walking along the streets spraining it in the process and I for sure was not the only one I heard too many stories of this happening to others it's not the hiking trails that you need to be afraid of it's the cobbled stones and as for the weather I found it to be super sunny and dry and Cusco the majority of the time it was glorious really in the daytime it is an absolutely perfect temperature in the low 20s you can comfortably walk around town in shorts and t-shirt however because of the altitude it gets very very chilly in the evening you want long trousers a base layer and a jacket I would get very chilly in the evenings in Cusco this goes for oil of Peru but I'd recommend getting cash out at the BCP ATMs they are the only ones I found that don't charge a withdrawal fee with the other Banks you can expect to pay around 18 solars per withdrawal you can use Uber to get around the city and if you do get a taxi I recommend having one that has been organized by your accommodation so you're not just trusting random vehicles on the street but if you're staying in the center you will barely have to be concerned about getting taxis because because everything is just walkable and anyway that's it this is the conclusion to my Ultimate Guide to Cusco I really hope that you guys have enjoyed this video please give it a thumbs up if you found it useful and subscribe to my channel for more Peru content and just more backpacking content around the world I'm now moving on from Cusco sadly in my next video and I will see you then bye thank you foreign
